
---
title: "ClinicalRWE: Oncology Real-World Evidence Study"
author: "Ludovic Njiosseu"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Executive Summary

This project evaluates whether preventive maintenance biologic therapy delays disease progression in a simulated oncology real-world evidence cohort while accounting for:
- treatment-selection bias
- competing discontinuation events
- missing clinical information
- socioeconomic confounding

The pipeline mirrors workflows used in oncology RWE, HEOR, and post-market comparative effectiveness studies.

# Research Question

Does receipt of preventive maintenance biologic therapy reduce progression risk after rigorous adjustment for:
- demographic imbalance
- treatment-selection bias
- missing data
- competing treatment discontinuation events?

# Cohort Construction

## Simulated Clinical Cohort

The cohort contains 8,000 oncology patients with:
- ECOG performance status
- biomarker positivity
- line of therapy
- Charlson comorbidity burden
- smoking status
- payer category
- socioeconomic indicators

Patients receiving preventive biologic therapy differ systematically from controls, generating realistic confounding structures for causal inference analyses.

## Clinical Endpoints

### Primary Endpoint
Time to disease progression.

### Competing Event
Treatment discontinuation due to adverse event or toxicity.

# Methods

## Module 1 — Clinical Cohort Construction

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

cohort <- read_csv("data/cohort.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

glimpse(cohort)
```

The cohort-generation pipeline simulates:
- confounded treatment assignment
- Weibull-distributed progression outcomes
- competing discontinuation events
- structured missingness mechanisms

# HEOR & Access Disparities

## Therapy Access Profiling

```{r}
therapy_disp <- read_csv("outputs/therapy_disparities.csv",
                         show_col_types = FALSE)

head(therapy_disp)
```

The HEOR module evaluates:
- therapy uptake disparities
- payer-related treatment-access inequities
- progression burden differences across demographic groups

## Cost Disparities

```{r}
cost_tbl <- read_csv("outputs/cost_disparities.csv",
                     show_col_types = FALSE)

cost_tbl
```

# Survival Analysis

## Kaplan-Meier Estimation

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("plots/km_therapy.png")
```

Methods included:
- Kaplan-Meier estimation
- log-rank testing
- Cox proportional hazards regression
- Schoenfeld residual diagnostics

## Cox Proportional Hazards Model

```{r}
cox_results <- read_csv("outputs/cox_results.csv",
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

cox_results
```

## Hazard Ratio Forest Plot

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("plots/cox_forest.png")
```

# Causal Inference

## Propensity Score Modeling

```{r}
ps <- read_csv("outputs/propensity_scores.csv",
               show_col_types = FALSE)

head(ps)
```

## Covariate Balance

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("plots/covariate_balance.png")
```

## IPTW / AIPW Estimation

```{r}
causal <- read_csv("outputs/causal_estimates.csv",
                   show_col_types = FALSE)

causal
```

The causal inference pipeline includes:
- positivity assessment
- stabilized IPTW weighting
- standardized mean difference evaluation
- doubly robust AIPW estimation
- E-value sensitivity analysis

# Missing Data

## Multiple Imputation

Structured MCAR/MAR missingness was introduced into:
- biomarker positivity
- BMI
- Charlson index
- poverty variables

Multiple imputation used:
- MICE
- predictive mean matching
- Rubin pooled inference

## MICE Comparison

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("plots/mice_compare.png")
```

```{r}
mice_tbl <- read_csv("outputs/complete_case_vs_mice.csv",
                     show_col_types = FALSE)

mice_tbl
```

# Competing Risks

## Fine-Gray Subdistribution Hazards

```{r}
comp_tbl <- read_csv(
  "outputs/competing_risk_model_comparison.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

comp_tbl
```

## Competing-Risk Comparison Plot

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics("plots/competing_risks.png")
```

The competing-risks framework distinguishes:
- disease progression
- treatment discontinuation

which is critical in oncology RWE analyses.

# Regulatory Relevance

This project mirrors workflows commonly used in:
- oncology RWE
- HEOR
- observational comparative effectiveness studies
- pharmacoepidemiology
- post-market evidence generation

Analyses are conceptually aligned with:
- FDA RWE guidance
- ICH E9(R1)
- estimand-oriented clinical research principles

# Conclusion

This project demonstrates a complete pharmaceutical RWE workflow integrating:
- survival analysis
- causal inference
- competing risks methodology
- multiple imputation
- HEOR disparity analysis
- reproducible analytics pipelines

within a portfolio-grade oncology evidence-generation framework.
